title: MCP UI rendering tool with browser broadcast and user interaction handling
purpose: Renders JSON UI definitions to browser clients via WebSocket and manages user interactions, supporting both blocking and non-blocking modes.
module_context: Exports UI validation function, render tool implementation, response handler, test utility, and tool schema definition for MCP protocol.

steps.content: Verify that the UI definition is a non-null object.
Confirm that the UI has a valid type property (non-empty string).
Ensure that the UI has a props property that is a valid object.
IF children exist in the UI, validate that they are an array of UI components.
IF actions exist in the UI, validate that they are an array with valid id and label properties.

methods.name: renderUI
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, ui: any, blocking: boolean, wsHandler: WebSocketHandler): Promise<RenderUIResponse>

steps.content: Validate that project and session parameters are non-empty strings.
Validate the structure of the UI component using the validation function.
Generate a unique identifier for this UI render operation.
Broadcast the UI definition to connected browser clients via WebSocket.
IF not in blocking mode, return a completion response immediately.
ELSE set up a promise that waits indefinitely for a user response.
Register a temporary handler to process incoming UI responses for this specific UI.
Validate that the incoming response is a non-null object.
Look up the registered handler for this UI component ID.
IF a handler exists and is callable, execute it with the response data.
Remove the handler from the registry after execution.
IF execution fails, log the error but continue processing.

methods.name: createUIResponse
methods.normalized_params: (uiId: string, actionId: string, data?: Record<string, any>): UIResponse

steps.content: Construct a UI response object with the provided component ID, action ID, and optional data.
Set the timestamp on the response to the current time.
